message:
fix(atc): fix duplicate SuspiciousEvidence blocks and simplify e-process factor clamping

- Gate 3: combine low-trusted-fraction and quarantined-evidence checks
  into a single condition to avoid emitting duplicate SuspiciousEvidence
  block reasons when both conditions hold simultaneously
- ConfidenceSequence::update: replace confusing copysign gymnastics with
  clean factor clamping to 1e-6. When 1+λZ ≤ 0 (catastrophically bad
  observation), the e-value correctly crashes toward zero via a large
  negative log update
- Gate 7: fix misleading comment ("worst" → "first sparse stratum found")

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-aazao.12.1","title":"Verification Coverage 5.1.1: verification ledger and CI shard design","description":"# Verification Coverage 5.1.1: Verification Ledger and CI Shard Design

## Why
The CI/doc branch needs a concrete planning bead for how the verification matrix will be represented and sliced. Without that, the coverage program will stay descriptive instead of enforceable.

## Deliverables
- define the machine-readable verification ledger or matrix format
- define suite shard boundaries and gating tiers
- map each major branch of the bead tree to the shard(s) that will enforce it

## Acceptance Criteria
- a future maintainer can see which jobs enforce which promises
- the verification tree has a clear execution model in CI rather than a flat pile of suites","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:16:48.190793628Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:16:48.190793628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","coverage","docs","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.12.1","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:16:48.190793628Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Diff-able between commits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:17:04.248532886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:17:04.248532886Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","coverage","ledger"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.12.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:17:04.248532886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.13","title":"Verification Coverage 5.2: final claim check and exception register for remaining non-real tests","description":"# Verification Coverage 5.2: Final Claim Check and Exception Register for Remaining Non-Real Tests

## Why
The whole point of this program is to make verification claims honest. At the end, the repo should have an explicit statement of what is truly covered, what still uses sanctioned substitutes, and why those exceptions remain.

## Deliverables
- produce a final claim check that answers:
  - what is covered by real-path unit tests
  - what is covered by realistic E2E tests
  - what still relies on substitutes, fixtures, mocks, or stubs
  - why those exceptions remain and what risk they imply
- produce an exception register with owner, rationale, and future-removal guidance for every remaining sanctioned substitute
- reject any vague “looks good” sign-off language in favor of specific claims tied to the verification ledger

## Acceptance Criteria
- future maintainers can answer the user’s original question quickly and truthfully from project artifacts rather than memory
- any remaining substitute-based coverage is visible and justified
- the project can distinguish “broad test count” from “credible coverage claim” in a defensible way

## Important Notes
This is the anti-hand-waving bead. If this cannot be completed honestly, the earlier beads are not done.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:53:19.215229439Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:53:19.215229439Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","repo-wide","signoff","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.13","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:53:19.215229439Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-aazao.14","title":"Verification Coverage 4.4: failure, recovery, stress, and performance E2E matrix completion","description":"# Verification Coverage 4.4: Failure, Recovery, Stress, and Performance E2E Matrix Completion

## Why
The existing suite surface does not yet justify a claim that the system is comprehensively tested under failure, restart, recovery, concurrency, or sustained load. Those are exactly the conditions where coordination software becomes dangerous if under-tested.

## Deliverables
- define and close an E2E matrix for restart/replay/recovery paths
- define and close an E2E matrix for concurrency, contention, and stress scenarios
- define and close an E2E matrix for dependency failures, degraded modes, and operator-visible recovery behavior
- ensure failure artifacts are rich enough to debug races, partial writes, retry loops, and degraded-service behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- the repo can point to explicit failure-path and stress-path suites rather than only happy-path workflows
- restart, retry, and degraded-state correctness are validated at the integration level
- performance-sensitive or race-sensitive paths have deliberate test coverage rather than folklore

## Important Notes
This is the hardening branch of the verification program. It exists to close the confidence gap between “works in the happy path” and “trustworthy under stress.”","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:58:29.085494431Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:58:29.085494431Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","failure","recovery","stress","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.14","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:58:29.085494431Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-aazao.2","title":"Verification Coverage 1.2: define policy for real-path tests, sanctioned substitutes, and evidence grades","description":"# Verification Coverage 1.2: Define Policy for Real-Path Tests, Sanctioned Substitutes, and Evidence Grades

## Why
The phrase “without mocks/fake stuff” is directionally right, but the repo needs a precise operational policy for what that means. Some external or nondeterministic dependencies may still require controlled substitutes, but those exceptions must be explicit, minimized, and never mistaken for full realism.

## Deliverables
- define evidence grades for tests (real-path, local-realistic fixture, sanctioned substitute, etc.)
- define which dependency classes must have real-path coverage somewhere in the stack:
  - SQLite / archive / filesystem / git
  - MCP stdio / HTTP transport
  - CLI binaries and install artifacts
  - TUI / robot / dashboard surfaces
  - search / embedding / LLM-related paths
  - share/export crypto and hosting flows
- define when a substitute is allowed and what additional real-path coverage is required to compensate
- define annotation / documentation rules for any remaining substitute-based tests
- define what “coverage closure” means for this repo so later sign-off is objective

## Acceptance Criteria
- no future contributor can point to a stub-only test and claim the real path is covered unless the policy explicitly permits that and identifies compensating coverage
- the policy is concrete enough to guide both unit-test design and E2E design
- sanctioned substitutes are treated as second-best evidence, not as silent equivalence to real execution

## Important Notes
This issue exists to stop future ambiguity. It should make later review disputes short and factual.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:13.704937382Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:53:30.484963797Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","policy","repo-wide","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:13.704937382Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:53:30.483615457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.2.1","title":"Verification Coverage 1.2.1: define evidence grades and test realism taxonomy","description":"# Verification Coverage 1.2.1: Define Evidence Grades and Test Realism Taxonomy

## Why
The repo needs a shared vocabulary for discussing what a test actually proves. Without that, “integration” and “real” become rhetorical instead of technical terms.

## Deliverables
- define the realism/evidence taxonomy used across the program
- provide examples for each grade taken from this repo
- define how the taxonomy applies across unit, conformance, and E2E lanes

## Acceptance Criteria
- review discussions can classify tests quickly and consistently
- later beads can reuse the taxonomy without redefining it","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:44.398489495Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:44.398489495Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","policy","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.2.1","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:44.398489495Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.2.2","title":"Verification Coverage 1.2.2: define sanctioned substitute policy and compensation rules","description":"# Verification Coverage 1.2.2: Define Sanctioned Substitute Policy and Compensation Rules

## Why
Some substitute-based tests may remain useful, but the repo needs explicit rules for when they are allowed and what real-path evidence must exist alongside them.

## Deliverables
- define allowed substitute classes and disallowed substitute classes
- define compensating real-path coverage requirements for allowed substitutes
- define annotation rules so substitute-based tests cannot masquerade as stronger evidence

## Acceptance Criteria
- substitute-based tests are deliberately justified rather than casually inherited
- reviewers can reject under-evidenced substitute usage on objective grounds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:55.610406489Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:55.610406489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","policy","realism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.2.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:01:55.610406489Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-aazao.4.1","title":"Verification Coverage 2.2.1: add search-core and DB search realism lanes","description":"# Verification Coverage 2.2.1: Add Search-Core and DB Search Realism Lanes

## Why
Search-core and DB search tests currently include stubbed engines or embedders that are appropriate for deterministic contracts but insufficient for stronger runtime claims.

## Deliverables
- separate deterministic contract tests from realism-oriented tests
- add locally realistic search/indexing lanes for critical flows
- document which behaviors still rely on substitutes and why

## Acceptance Criteria
- search behavior claims are backed by at least one realism-oriented lane for each critical path
- deterministic stub tests are clearly scoped as such","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:00.642282154Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:00.642282154Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","realism","search","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:00.642282154Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.4.2","title":"Verification Coverage 2.2.2: add LLM and summarization realism lanes with explicit labeling","description":"# Verification Coverage 2.2.2: Add LLM and Summarization Realism Lanes with Explicit Labeling

## Why
LLM-adjacent tests are especially easy to over-interpret. The repo needs to distinguish deterministic adapter tests from evidence about real summarization or completion behavior.

## Deliverables
- inventory current stubbed LLM/summarization tests
- define which of those remain contract-only lanes
- add realism-oriented lanes or explicit compensating evidence for critical LLM-adjacent behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- LLM-adjacent behavior is not represented as fully proven by stubs alone
- remaining stub-based tests are labeled and compensated intentionally","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:26.025346430Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:26.025346430Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","llm","realism","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:05:26.025346430Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.5","title":"Verification Coverage 3.1: core, DB, storage, and guard unit-coverage closure on real state transitions","description":"# Verification Coverage 3.1: Core, DB, Storage, and Guard Unit-Coverage Closure on Real State Transitions

## Why
These crates hold the system’s state machines, persistence guarantees, archive behavior, reservation enforcement, timestamp handling, and failure recovery logic. They need the strongest non-hand-wavy unit and integration coverage in the entire workspace.

## Deliverables
- close unit-test gaps in `mcp-agent-mail-core` for configuration, models, timestamps, evidence/experience state machines, ATC-related contracts, and utility invariants
- close unit and integration gaps in `mcp-agent-mail-db` for queries, migrations, transitions, search state, retry/consistency behavior, and corruption handling
- close unit and integration gaps in `mcp-agent-mail-storage` for archive writes, coalescing, git-side effects, stale-lock handling, and crash/retry safety
- close unit gaps in `mcp-agent-mail-guard` for reservation enforcement, rename handling, overlap detection, and archive-read semantics
- prefer real DB / filesystem / repo operations wherever feasible rather than purely fabricated stand-ins

## Acceptance Criteria
- all critical state transitions and persistence boundaries have happy-path, edge-case, and failure-path tests
- no high-risk DB/archive/guard behavior is validated only by shallow smoke tests or synthetic-only assertions
- the resulting tests make it realistic to trust later E2E failures as integration bugs rather than obvious missing unit coverage

## Important Notes
This is foundational verification work. If this bead is weak, the entire rest of the coverage program becomes noisy and expensive.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":480,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:46.052331214Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:54:50.601173012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coverage","db","guard","storage","testing","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5","depends_on_id":"br-aazao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:51:46.052331214Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:54:29.227040467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:54:39.912690952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:54:50.599976979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-aazao.5.1","title":"timestamps.rs: unit tests for conversion functions and boundary values","description":"timestamps.rs (181 lines) has ZERO unit tests. Tests needed:
- naive_to_micros(epoch=0) == 0
- naive_to_micros(known datetime) matches hand-calculated microseconds
- micros_to_naive(0) == 1970-01-01T00:00:00
- micros_to_naive(negative) uses div_euclid/rem_euclid correctly for pre-1970
- micros_to_naive(i64::MAX) and (i64::MIN) hit the chrono overflow fallback paths
- naive_to_micros(micros_to_naive(x)) roundtrip for 20+ values spanning full range
- micros_to_iso format matches '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%.6fZ'
- iso_to_micros parses rfc3339 with timezone
- iso_to_micros parses naive with trailing Z
- iso_to_micros parses naive without Z
- iso_to_micros parses seconds-only (no fractional)
- iso_to_micros returns None for garbage input
- iso_to_micros(micros_to_iso(x)) roundtrip
All real chrono operations, no mocks.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T13:59:54.545136798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:59:54.545136798Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","real-path","timestamps","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:59:54.545136798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-aazao.5.2","title":"timestamps.rs: unit tests for now_micros clock skew protection, monotonicity, and concurrency","description":"Tests for the clock skew protection subsystem in timestamps.rs:
- now_micros() called 1000x sequentially → all values strictly increasing (monotonicity)
- now_micros() from 10 concurrent threads → collect into BTreeSet, verify zero duplicates
- clock_skew_metrics() returns ClockSkewMetrics with sane defaults after reset
- clock_skew_reset() zeros backward_jumps, forward_jumps, and last_system_time_us
- Verify LAST_SYSTEM_TIME_US.fetch_add path: after setting high-water mark artificially high, next now_micros() returns prev+1 (backward clock branch)
- now_micros_raw() returns a value within 1s of now_micros()
- After clock_skew_reset(), verify counters start fresh
All real atomics and real system clock. No mocking of time.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:00:25.824265669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:00:25.824265669Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","core","real-path","timestamps","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:00:25.824265669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-aazao.5.3","title":"storage: integration tests for archive write → git verify → stale lock recovery cycle","description":"Granular integration tests for the full archive pipeline:
- Write single message → verify file exists on disk in correct date-partitioned path
- Write message → verify git log shows commit with expected content
- Write with artificial stale .git/index.lock (dead PID) → verify automatic recovery and successful write
- Concurrent 5-thread writes → verify commit count reduced via coalescer (batching ratio > 1.0)
- Write → read back via archive reader → verify content roundtrip
- Write agent profile → verify agents/ directory structure
- Write file reservation artifact → verify file_reservations/ path
All real git repos in tempfile::TempDir.  {"id":"br-aazao.5.4","title":"guard: edge-case unit tests for reservation patterns, overlaps, and rename semantics","description":"Edge-case tests for mcp-agent-mail-guard (has 82+25 tests, needs edge expansion):
- Deeply nested glob: a/**/b/**/c.rs matches a/x/b/y/c.rs
- Overlapping exclusive+shared: agent A has exclusive src/**, agent B requests shared src/foo.rs → conflict
- Expired reservation vs active: expired exclusive should not block new exclusive
- Rename detection: reservation on old_path.rs, file renamed to new_path.rs → guard behavior
- Empty paths list → no-op, no error
- Single file vs directory glob: src/lib.rs vs src/**
- Case sensitivity: SRC/lib.rs vs src/lib.rs
- Reservation on path outside repo root → appropriate error
All real fnmatch-regex evaluation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:00:48.469867154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:24:46.291838585Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["guard","real-path","reservations","unit-test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:24:46.291121011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:00:48.469867154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.5.5","title":"Verification Coverage 3.1.5: core config, models, and evidence-state invariant coverage","description":"# Verification Coverage 3.1.5: Core Config, Models, and Evidence-State Invariant Coverage

## Why
The 3.1 branch now has timestamp, storage, and guard beads, but it still needs a dedicated core bead so config parsing, model validation, naming rules, and evidence-state invariants do not get treated as incidental coverage.

## Deliverables
- add tests for environment/config parsing invariants
- add tests for model validation and naming invariants
- add tests for evidence/experience/metrics invariants that are easy to regress silently

## Acceptance Criteria
- the core crate is not represented mainly by timestamp coverage alone
- high-blast-radius core invariants have nearby tests and an explicit bead owner","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:09:38.396562395Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:09:38.396562395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","coverage","testing","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.5","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:09:38.396562395Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-aazao.5.6","title":"Verification Coverage 3.1.6: DB query, migration, FTS, and corruption-handling coverage","description":"# Verification Coverage 3.1.6: DB Query, Migration, FTS, and Corruption-Handling Coverage

## Why
The SQLite layer needs a dedicated closure bead beyond storage-path coverage because subtle query, migration, FTS, cache, and corruption behaviors are easy to miss until they fail in production.

## Deliverables
- add direct tests for critical queries and write/read transitions
- add migration and schema-evolution coverage
- add FTS/search-state coverage with realistic fixtures
- add corruption, retry, and recovery-path coverage where feasible

## Acceptance Criteria
- DB correctness claims are backed by targeted tests rather than inferred from higher-level workflows alone
- the audit can point to a dedicated closure bead for the DB layer","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-03-18T14:10:01.454708065Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","updated_at":"2026-03-18T14:10:01.454708065Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","db","fts","testing","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-aazao.5.6","depends_on_id":"br-aazao.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T14:10:01.454708065Z","created_by":"CrimsonSnow","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
